question: Among Providence hospital properties, only one met the following criteria: it had an assessed value consistently between $6.9 million and $7.3 million from 2015 through 2018, and it is neither an Ivy League university nor named after an incorporated Rhode Island city. The benefactor associated with this property was named in honor of another person. Who was that person's grandfather?. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: In 2015, the 9 Providence properties with total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million were: 000-0001-0003 (City of Providence, $7.00M), 016-0633-0000 (Brown University, $6.99M), 023-0772-LL01 (RI Hospital, $7.05M), 004-0261-0000 (261 LLC, $7.25M), 024-0613-0000 (Gilbane Building, $7.09M), 025-0346-REVO (Johnson and Wales University, $6.96M), 033-0381-0000 (Housing Authority, $7.05M), 034-0381-0000 (Calvi Realty, $7.14M), and 068-0638-0000 (Housing Authority, $6.96M).
1.subquestion: Which Providence properties had total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million in 2015?

2.fact: In 2016, the 18 Providence properties with total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million included: 000-0001-0003 (City of Providence), 016-0633-0000 (Brown University), 023-0772-LL01 (RI Hospital), and 15 other properties including Brown University, RI Hospital, Hospital Properties Inc, and various commercial entities.
2.subquestion: Which Providence properties had total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million in 2016?

3.fact: In 2017, the 19 Providence properties with total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million included: 000-0001-0003 (City of Providence), 016-0633-0000 (Brown University), 023-0772-LL01 (RI Hospital), and 16 other properties.
3.subquestion: Which Providence properties had total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million in 2017?

4.fact: In 2018, the 21 Providence properties with total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million included: 000-0001-0003 (City of Providence, $7.00M), 016-0633-0000 (Brown University, $7.25M), 023-0772-LL01 (RI Hospital, $7.05M), and 18 other properties.
4.subquestion: Which Providence properties had total assessment between $6.9 million and $7.3 million in 2018?

5.fact: The national-incorporated-places dataset lists incorporated places in the United States by name, state, and class code. 'City of Providence' (Rhode Island, Class C5) is listed as an incorporated city. Neither 'RI Hospital' nor 'Brown University' appear as incorporated places in the dataset.
5.subquestion: Of the three properties in the intersection (City of Providence, RI Hospital, Brown University), which have owner names that match incorporated places in Rhode Island according to the national-incorporated-places dataset?

6.fact: Brown University is a private Ivy League research university in Providence, Rhode Island. It was founded in 1764 and is one of nine colonial colleges chartered before the American Revolution.
6.subquestion: Is the property owned by 'Brown University' an Ivy League university?
6.answer: Yes, Brown University is an Ivy League university

7.fact: Moses Brown Ives was a businessman and philanthropist from Providence, Rhode Island who was named after Moses Brown. Upon his death, he created a bequest which was used to provide some of the early funding for the creation of Rhode Island Hospital in 1863.
7.subquestion: Who created a bequest that provided early funding for Rhode Island Hospital?

8.fact: Moses Brown (September 23, 1738 - September 6, 1836) was a Rhode Island Quaker, abolitionist, and philanthropist. He was the co-founder of Brown University. His grandfather was Baptist minister James Brown (1666-1732), one of the earliest settlers of Providence.
8.subquestion: Who was the grandfather of Moses Brown, after whom <node_7 answer> was named?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Providence properties with $6.9M-$7.3M assessment in 2015 -> 9 properties
Node 2: Providence properties with $6.9M-$7.3M assessment in 2016 -> 18 properties
Node 3: Providence properties with $6.9M-$7.3M assessment in 2017 -> 19 properties
Node 4: Providence properties with $6.9M-$7.3M assessment in 2018 -> 21 properties
Intersection of nodes 1-4: Properties meeting criterion all 4 years -> {City of Providence, RI Hospital, Brown University}
HOP 2 (d=2, mixed data.gov + Wikipedia - nodes 5-6 filter the intersection):
Node 5 (data.gov): Check if owner name matches incorporated RI city -> only 'City of Providence' matches
Filter: Owner NOT an incorporated RI city -> {RI Hospital, Brown University}
Node 6 (Wikipedia): Brown University -> IS an Ivy League university
Filter: NOT an Ivy League university -> {City of Providence, RI Hospital}
Intersection of filters: {RI Hospital, Brown University} AND {City of Providence, RI Hospital} -> {RI Hospital}
Node 7: Moses Brown Ives created a bequest that provided early funding for Rhode Island Hospital and was named after Moses Brown
Node 8: Moses Brown (after whom Moses Brown Ives was named) had grandfather James Brown (1666-1732)
